payload.title: R4001 false positive on local fn names colliding with std seed (e.g. local fn read)
payload.description: rustricted-effects' check pass uses simple-name lookup against (declared | seed). A user crate that defines fn read(s: &str) -> String (pure) and calls it from a fn that declares no effects fires R4001 demanding 'io' — because the call collector grabs the simple name 'read', misses the local-no-effects entry, falls through to the seed where 'read' is hardcoded as io. Repro in case-studies/effects-audit.md ("Latent bugs" section #1). If RT-10 picks kill, this becomes moot. If RT-10 keeps effects, the fix is to make the seed table receiver-type-aware OR exclude any name that also appears as a local fn definition.
